LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS THE GYMBOREE CORPORATION, JOHN SHUNK'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS IS CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 6, 2010 ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. JUDGE: PAUL H. ALVARADO, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS THE GYMBOREE CORPORATION AND JOHN SHUNK'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - GRANTED, THIS CIVIL CASE IS STAYED PENDING COMPLETION OF ARBITRATION. THE PARTIES FORMED A VALID AND ENFORCEABLE AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE THAT ENCOMPASSES THIS DISPUTE. THE "ANY AND ALL PAST OR PRESENT DISPUTES WITH ANYONE "LANGUAGE CAN BE FAIRLY READ TO INCLUDE ALL DISPUTES ARISING FROM THE EMPLOYMENT RELATIONSHIP WHEN THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE IS READ IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE AGREEMENT WAS ENTERED INTO AT THE BEGINNING OF PLAINTIFF'S EMPLOYMENT. ANY DOUBTS OR AMBIGUITIES AS TO THE SCOPE OF THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE ITSELF SHOULD BE RESOLVED IN FAVOR OF ARBITRATION. CRONUS INVESTMENTS, INC. V. CONCIERGE SERVICES (2005) 35 CAL.4TH 376, 386. THE AGREEMENT WAS NOT UNENFORCEABLE BECAUSE PLAINTIFF WAS REQUIRED TO SIGN IT AS A TERM OF HER EMPLOYMENT. LAGATREE V. LUCE, FORWARD, HAMILTON & SCRIPPS (1999) 74 CAL.APP.4TH 1105. THE FACT THAT THE CAA RULES ARE INCORPORATED BUT NOT ATTACHED MAY INDICATE SOME PROCEDURAL UNCONSCIONABILITY BUT DOES NOT MAKE THE AGREEMENT UNENFORCEABLE. THE CAA RULES ACTUALLY FAVOR THE EMPLOYEE AND PROVIDE PROCEDURAL SAFEGUARDS. SLIGHT PROCEDURAL UNCONSCIONABILITY WOULD REQUIRE A MORE OPPRESIVE SUBSTANTIVE UNCONSCIONABILITY TO RENDER THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT UNENFORCEABLE. NO SUBSTANTIVE UNCONSCIONABILITY IS PRESENT HERE. THE AGREEMENT PROVIDES ALL OF THE SAFEGUARDS REQUIRED BY ARMENDARIZ, AND IS MUTUALLY BINDING ON BOTH PARTIES. BOTH PARTIES ARE PERMITTED TO APPLY FOR CIVIL INJUNCTIVE RELIEF AND DOES NOT EXEMPT CLAIMS FROM ARBITRATION THAT ARE ONLY LIKELY TO BE BROUGHT BY THE EMPLOYER, SUCH AS INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY VIOLATIONS. MCMANUS V. CIBC WORLD MARKETS, CORP. (2003) 109 CAL.APP.4TH 76. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER). JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
